canonical_definition: Based on the available data, MathTutorBench is presented as a benchmark for measuring the open-ended pedagogical capabilities of LLMs in the domain of mathematics tutoring. The instrument is defined as involving task prompts, human evaluation, and metrics specifically focused on scaffolding. Its stated purpose is to evaluate certain abilities of LLM tutors. In particular, MathTutorBench is used to operationalize and measure the construct of domain-specific knowledge. The provided evidence specifies that one of its evaluation categories is "math expertise which evaluates the subject-matter expertise of the tutor."
papers: Gradient-Attention Guided Dual-Masking Synergetic Framework for Robust Text-based Person Retrieval
Is the Top Still Spinning? Evaluating Subjectivity in Narrative Understanding

definitions.text: A benchmark for measuring open-ended pedagogical capabilities of LLMs in mathematics tutoring, involving task prompts, human evaluation, and scaffolding-focused metrics.
